title: server_fail() returns raw SQLite error strings in JMAP response
description: In jmap.rs, server_fail(msg) returns the description field verbatim from the caller, and most call sites pass e.to_string() where e is a sqlx::Error. SQLite error strings contain table names, column names, and constraint names (e.g. 'UNIQUE constraint failed: messages.space_id, messages.channel_id'). A token holder who triggers an error path receives detailed schema information in the JMAP response body. Fix: log the full error server-side, return only a generic 'serverFail' type with no description to the client, or return a sanitized message without schema details.
